text.title: CPA Rollup Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes that make the rollup mislead budget allocation. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: Double-counting — conversion attributed to multiple channels because dedup ID is missing or unstable.
failure-mode.detector: Sum of attributed_conversions across channels > actual unique conversions in CRM
failure-mode.repair: Audit identity-resolution: ensure customer ID is stable across sessions, hashed consistently, and present on every touchpoint. Re-run rollup.

failure-mode.description: Agency fees and creative production costs excluded — CPA looks artificially low.
failure-mode.detector: gross_spend equals ad-platform billing only AND agency invoice exists for same period
failure-mode.repair: Add fees to gross_spend per r2. Re-evaluate channel viability — some channels flip from "winning" to "losing" once fees are included.

failure-mode.description: View-through count added to attributed conversions to "make Meta CPA look better."
failure-mode.detector: attributed_conversions per channel exceeds what the attribution model alone would produce AND view_through column is empty
failure-mode.repair: Separate view-through into its own column. Report CPA with click-only attribution. If view-through is critical to the business, fund an incrementality test to quantify it.

failure-mode.description: Branded-search paid cannibalizes organic — Google paid CPA looks great but conversions would have happened on organic.
failure-mode.detector: Branded keywords drive >= 30% of Google paid conversions AND incremental_over_organic for those keywords is near zero
failure-mode.repair: Carve branded vs non-branded into separate rows. Apply incrementality lens. Consider pausing branded paid (geo-test if uncertain).

failure-mode.description: Cohort retention column not populated — channel allocation rewards low-quality acquisition.
failure-mode.detector: cohort_d30 column blank across > 3 channels for cohorts older than 30 days
failure-mode.repair: Wire the cohort retention computation. If CRM doesn't track activity, define minimum viable signal (login, action, purchase). Cohort lens is what catches mercenary channels.

failure-mode.description: Attribution policy quietly changed mid-quarter ("last click within 7 days" became "linear across 30 days") — historical comparison invalid.
failure-mode.detector: attribution-policy.md modified more than once per quarter AND historical rollup not re-run with new model
failure-mode.repair: Treat attribution changes like an experiment: version the model, re-run the last 90 days under both, document the delta, brief the team. Don't change silently.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: Channel-vendor dashboards may include narrative ("Meta lifted incremental conversions by 30%") that is not evidence. The rollup uses raw spend/conversion data, not vendor narratives.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Weekly review by growth lead + finance partner. The rollup is the artifact; the conversation is the gate. Budget shifts > 20% across channels require two-week confirmation before commitment.
